# Clarian Finance API

> Payment gateway for Brazil/LATAM. Receive BRL via PIX (cash-in), hold it in a
> per-workspace ledger balance, and withdraw it via PIX (cash-out) to a
> pre-registered account. Also: BRL<->stablecoin quotes, on-chain wallet reads,
> and signed webhooks. This file is self-contained — enough to integrate without
> other docs. Machine-readable spec: /openapi.yaml. Official SDK: @clarian-finance/sdk.

## Core model

- A workspace (partner) is identified by `X-Workspace-Id` (a UUID) and holds a
  **BRL ledger balance** — this is the partner's account. Cash-in credits it,
  cash-out debits it. It is NOT a crypto wallet (those are separate, on-chain).
- You can only withdraw the balance you actually received via your own cash-ins,
  and only to your workspace's **registered settlement PIX key**.

## Base URLs

- Production: `https://api.clarian.finance/functions/v1/api-gateway/live`
- Sandbox:    `https://api.clarian.finance/functions/v1/api-gateway/test`

The key prefix must match the segment: `cl_live_sk_...` on `/live`, `cl_test_sk_...` on `/test`.
Sandbox (`/test`) has a simulated PIX rail plus sandbox-only helpers (simulate, webhook test/resend).

## Authentication (every request)

    Authorization: Bearer cl_live_sk_<secret>
    X-Workspace-Id: <workspace-uuid>

Production keys also enforce an **IP allowlist** — register your server IPs in the
dashboard, or requests return `403 ip_not_whitelisted`.

## Idempotency

Send `Idempotency-Key: <unique-per-intent>` on mutating calls. A retry with the
same key returns the original result and never double-executes. **Required for
cash-out** (`400 idempotency_key_required` if missing).

## Errors

Non-2xx returns `{ "error": "<code>", "hint": "<optional>" }`. Common codes:
`missing_or_malformed_authorization`, `invalid_key`, `ip_not_whitelisted`,
`workspace_mismatch`, `rate_limited` (120 req/min per key), `invalid_amount`,
`insufficient_balance` (402), `settlement_key_required` (403),
`idempotency_key_required`, `provider_error` (502).

## Endpoints

### GET /ping
Auth probe. → `{ ok, environment, master_account_id, scope, customer_id }`

### POST /pix/payins — receive BRL (generate a PIX charge)
Request:

    { "amount": 250.00,
      "payer": { "name": "Maria Silva", "document": { "number": "12345678900" } },
      "description": "Order #123",        // optional
      "expiration_seconds": 3600,          // optional, default 86400
      "customer_id": "<uuid>" }            // optional: credit a sub-account

Response `201`:

    { "ok": true, "environment": "production",
      "order": { "id": "<uuid>",            // use this with GET /cash-in/:id
                 "status": "pending",
                 "amount": 250,
                 "currency": "BRL",
                 "pix": { "qr_code": "<base64 png>",
                          "copy_paste": "00020101...br.gov.bcb.pix...",
                          "end_to_end_id": null },
                 "fee": 0 } }

Show `pix.copy_paste` (EMV / copia-e-cola) and/or render `pix.qr_code`. When the
payer pays, you receive a `pix_payin.completed` webhook and the ledger is credited
net of fee.

### GET /cash-in/:id
Fetch a cash-in by the `order.id` UUID. → `{ ok, environment, order }`.

### POST /pix/payouts/dict — preview a PIX key's owner (before paying out)
Request: `{ "pix_key": "maria@example.com", "key_type": "EMAIL" }`  (key_type optional: CPF|CNPJ|EMAIL|PHONE|EVP)
Response: `{ ok, environment, dict: { name, document: { type, number }, keyType, ... } }`

### POST /pix/payouts — withdraw BRL (send PIX)
Requires `Idempotency-Key`. If your workspace has a registered settlement key,
send ONLY the amount — the destination is fixed to that key:

    { "amount": 100.00, "description": "Withdrawal" }

Response `201`:

    { "ok": true, "environment": "production",
      "order": { "id": "<uuid>",
                 "status": "pending",
                 "end_to_end_id": "<gowd ref, once known>",
                 "amount": 100,          // requested
                 "pix_amount": 98,        // actually sent (after end_customer fee)
                 "currency": "BRL",
                 "fee": 2,
                 "fee_bearer": "end_customer" } }

Refused with `403 settlement_key_required` if no settlement PIX key is registered,
`402 insufficient_balance` if the ledger balance is too low. On `pix_payout.completed`
webhook the payout settled; on failure the reserved funds are released.

### GET /cash-out/:id
Fetch a cash-out by the `order.id` UUID.

### GET /account/balances
→ `{ ok, environment, balances: [ { currency: "BRL", available, pending, locked } ] }`

### GET /transactions?type=&status=&limit=
`type` ∈ pix_in | pix_out | on_ramp_deposit | off_ramp_payout. → `{ ok, environment, transactions: [...] }`
### GET /transactions/:id → `{ ok, environment, transaction }`

### GET /wallets  and  GET /wallets/:id/balance
On-chain (Turnkey) wallets — crypto, separate from the BRL ledger.

### POST /rfq/quote  and  POST /rfq/execute
BRL <-> USDT/USDC quotes. quote: `{ base_currency, quote_currency, amount, amount_currency }`
→ `{ quote_id, rate, base_size, quote_size, expires_at }`; execute: `{ quote_id }`.

### Webhooks CRUD  (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /webhooks[/:id])
Create: `{ "url": "https://you.example.com/clarian", "events": ["pix_payin.completed","pix_payout.completed"] }`
→ `{ ok, environment, subscription: { id, url, events, ... }, secret: "whsec_..." }`
The `secret` is shown ONCE — store it. URLs must be public `https://` (internal/loopback hosts are rejected).

### Sandbox-only helpers (require `cl_test_sk_` / `/test` segment; else `404 sandbox_only`)

### POST /pix/payins/:id/simulate
Body: `{ "status"?: "completed"|"expired"|"failed" }` (default completed)
→ `200 { ok, environment, order }`

### POST /pix/payouts/:id/simulate
Body: `{ "status"?: "completed"|"failed" }` (default completed)
→ `200 { ok, environment, order }`

Magic sandbox PIX keys for payouts: `fail@sandbox.clarian` (fails + refund),
`pending@sandbox.clarian` (stays pending until simulated).

### POST /webhooks/:id/test
Body: `{ "event_type": "pix_payin.completed" }` (sample types: pix_payin.created|completed|expired,
pix_payout.created|completed|failed, checkout.paid)
→ `202 { ok, environment, event_type, enqueued: { enqueued, delivery_id } }`

### POST /webhooks/deliveries/:id/resend
→ `202 { ok, delivery_id }`

SDK: `clarian.sandbox.simulateCashIn(id, status?)`, `simulateCashOut(id, status?)`,
`sendWebhookEvent(subscriptionId, eventType)`, `resendWebhookDelivery(deliveryId)`.
Also: `signWebhookPayload(body, timestamp, secret)` (hex HMAC for local handler tests),
`SANDBOX_FAIL_PIX_KEY`, `SANDBOX_PENDING_PIX_KEY`.

## Receiving webhooks (verify EVERY delivery)

Delivery headers: `X-Clarian-Signature` (hex HMAC-SHA256), `X-Clarian-Timestamp`,
`X-Clarian-Event`, `X-Clarian-Delivery-Id`, `X-Clarian-Idempotency-Key`.
The signature is `HMAC_SHA256(secret, timestamp + "." + rawBody)` (hex).
Verify: recompute over the EXACT raw body, constant-time compare, and reject if the
timestamp is older than ~5 minutes (replay protection). Dedupe on the delivery id.
Event types include: `pix_payin.completed`, `pix_payin.expired`, `pix_payout.completed`,
`pix_payout.failed`, `checkout.completed`, `checkout.expired`, `checkout.failed`,
`rfq.executed`, `rfq.failed`.

## SDK (recommended)

    npm install github:clarian-finance/sdk

    import { Clarian, constructWebhookEvent, extractWebhookHeaders } from "@clarian-finance/sdk";

    const clarian = new Clarian({
      apiKey: process.env.CLARIAN_API_KEY!,        // cl_live_sk_... or cl_test_sk_...
      workspaceId: process.env.CLARIAN_WORKSPACE_ID!,
    });

    // Receive BRL
    const deposit = await clarian.cashIn.create({
      amount: 250,
      payer: { name: "Maria", document: { number: "12345678900" } },
    });
    console.log(deposit.pix.copy_paste);

    // Withdraw BRL (idempotency key required)
    const payout = await clarian.cashOut.create(
      { amount: 100 },
      "withdrawal-2026-07-01-001",
    );

    // Balances
    const balances = await clarian.balances.list();

    // Verify a webhook (Express-style handler with the raw body)
    const headers = extractWebhookHeaders(req.headers);
    const event = await constructWebhookEvent(rawBody, headers, webhookSecret);
    // event.event, event.data

Errors throw `ClarianError` with `.status`, `.code`, `.detail`.
